examples: The window had opened easily at his touch, belying the complex lock that had once existed there.
Gordon Brown, belying his clunking image, has brought tech-savvy communications staff into Downing Street.
Power quality ratings are now in the top half, belying the image of junky Korean cars.
Royals are often met with scepticism among the Brits, belying the reverence they get overseas.
Central Asia, belying its label, is on the edge of this space politically as well as physically.
